1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: I CALL TO WITNESS those who are sent consecutively,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: By oath of those that are sent, one after the other. (The verses of the Holy Qur’an or the angels or the winds).

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: By the winds sent forth with beneficence.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: By the winds (or angels or the Messengers of Allah) sent forth one after another.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: By the (winds) sent forth in quick succession,

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: By the emissary winds, (sent) one after another

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: By (the wind) those sent (as horses') mane (in succession),

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: By those [winds] sent forth in gusts

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: By (the angels) sent forth with the commands of God,

1.author: Shakir
1.text: I swear by the emissary winds, sent one after another (for men's benefit),

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: By the winds sent forth in swift succession,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: By the (Winds) sent forth one after another (to man's profit);

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Then by oath of those that push with a strong gust.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And by the winds that blow violently,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: by those who sweep along like gale,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And the winds that blow violently

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: by (the angels) as swift as blowing winds,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: and then storming on with a tempest's force,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Which then blow violently in tempestuous Gusts,

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: And those that revive by quickening,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Then by oath of those that lift and carry.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And by the winds that scatter clouds and rain;

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and raise (clouds) and scatter them around,

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: By those which cause earth's vegetation to revive;

3.author: Qarai
3.text: by those who publish [the Divine messages] far and wide,

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: by (the angels) spreading (the words of God) far and wide,

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Which scatter clouds to their destined places,

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Then by those that clearly differentiate the right and wrong.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And by the Verses (of the Quran) that separate the right from the wrong.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: By those who winnow with a winnowing,

4.author: Qarai
4.text: by those who separate [the truth from falsehood] distinctly,

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: then the criterion (the verses of Koran), separating

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: by (the angels) who make a clear distinction between right and wrong

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Then separate them one from another,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Then separate them, one from another,

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: And those that bring down the Reminder

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: And then by those that instil Remembrance into the hearts.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And those winds that bring down the remembrance.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And by the angels that bring the revelations to the Messengers,

5.author: Maududi
5.text: and then cast (Allah's) remembrance (in people's hearts),

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: By those who bring down the Reminder,

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And those [angels] who deliver a message

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: and by those who reveal revelations (to the prophets)

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Then I swear by the angels who bring down the revelation,

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: by those who bring down the reminder,

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: To end all argument or to warn.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: To complete the argument or to warn.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: By way of excuse or warning.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: To cut off all excuses or to warn;

6.author: Maududi
6.text: to serve as an excuse or a warning.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: to provide excuses for some and to give warnings to others:

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: to excuse some and warn others:

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Whether of Justification or of Warning;-

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: What is promised will surely come to pass.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Indeed what you are promised, will surely befall.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: surely that which you are promised is about to fall!

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Verily that which ye are promised is about to befall.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Surely, what you are promised must come to pass.

7.author: Itani
7.text: Surely what you are promised will happen.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: Surely what you are promised shall come to pass.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Surely, what you are promised will occur.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Surely that which ye are promised will befall.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: what you are promised will surely befall.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: surely, that which you have been promised is about to fall!

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Indeed, what you are promised is to occur.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: that whatever with which you have been warned will inevitably come to pass.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Most surely what you are threatened with must come to pass.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: that which you have been promised shall be fulfilled.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Assuredly, what ye are promised must come to pass.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: So when the lights of the stars are put out.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: When the stars shall be extinguished,

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Then when the stars lose their lights;

8.author: Maududi
8.text: So when the stars are extinguished,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Then when the stars lose their lights.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: So when the stars are put out,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: When the stars are blotted out

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: So when the stars are obliterated

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Then the stars will lose their light.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: So when the stars are made to lose their light,

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: When the stars lose their light,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Then when the stars become dim;

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And when the sky is split apart.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And when the heaven is cloven asunder.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And when the heaven is cleft asunder;

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and the sky is rent asunder,

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And when the sky is riven asunder,

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: and when the sky is rent asunder

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And when the heaven is opened

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And when the heaven is rent asunder,

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: and when the sky is rent asunder,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: When the heaven is cleft asunder;

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: The mountains reduced to dust and blown away,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And when the mountains are made into dust and blown away.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: when the mountains shall be scattered

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And when the mountains are carried away by wind.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And when the mountains are blown away;

10.author: Itani
10.text: And the mountains are blown away.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: and the mountains are blown away,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And when the mountains are blown away.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And when the mountains are blown away,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: when the mountains are scattered [like dust]

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And when the mountains are blown away

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: The mountains will be blown away as dust.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And when the mountains are carried away as dust,

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: and when the mountains crumble into dust

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: When the mountains are scattered (to the winds) as dust;

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: And when the time comes for raising the little girls (buried alive) --

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: And when the time of the Noble Messengers arrives.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: and when the Messengers' time is set,

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And when the apostles are collected at the appointed time,

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: And when the Messengers are gathered to their time appointed;

11.author: Maududi
11.text: and the appointed time to bring the Messengers together arrives, (then shall the promised event come to pass).

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: And when the Messengers are Uqqitat.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: And when the messengers are brought unto their time appointed -

11.author: Qarai
11.text: and time is set for the apostles [to bear witness]

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: and when the Messengers' time is set

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And when the messengers' time has come...

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: The Messengers will receive their appointments.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And when the apostles are gathered at their appointed time

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: and when the messengers are brought together at the appointed time --

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: And when the messengers are (all) appointed a time (to collect);-

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: For what day is that time fixed?

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: For which day were they appointed?

12.author: Arberry
12.text: to what day shall they be delayed?

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: For what day is it timed?

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: For what Day are these signs postponed?

12.author: Itani
12.text: Until which day is it deferred?

12.author: Maududi
12.text: To which Day has this task been deferred?

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: For what Day are these signs postponed

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: For what day is the time appointed?

12.author: Qarai
12.text: —for what day has [all] that been set [to occur]?

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: to what day shall they be deferred?

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: For what Day was it postponed?

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: If one asks, "To which day have such calamitous events been postponed?"

12.author: Shakir
12.text: To what day is the doom fixed?

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: for what Day has this been appointed?

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: For what Day are these (portents) deferred?

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: For the Day of sorting out (the men of Paradise from the men destined for Hell).

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: For the Day of Sorting Out (the men of Paradise from the men destined for Hell).

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: one will be told,"To the Day of Distinction".

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: For the Day of Sorting out.

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: How will you comprehend what the Day of Judgement is?

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And what do you know, what the Day of Decision is!

14.author: Arberry
14.text: And what shall teach thee what is the Day of Decision?

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And what knowest thou what the Day of Decision is?

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And what will explain to you what is the Day of sorting out?

14.author: Itani
14.text: And what will teach you what the Day of Decision is?

14.author: Maududi
14.text: What do you know what the Day of Judgement is?

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And what will explain to you what is the Day of Sorting Out

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And what will convey unto thee what the Day of Decision is! -

14.author: Qarai
14.text: And what will show you what is the Day of Judgement!?

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: Would that you knew what the Day of Decision is!

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And what can make you know what is the Day of Judgement?

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: Would that you knew about the Day of Judgment!

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And what will make you comprehend what the day of decision is?

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: What will explain to you what the Day of Judgement is?

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And what will explain to thee what is the Day of Sorting out?

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

15.author: Itani
15.text: Woe on that Day to the liars.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: On that day, woe will be to those who have rejected God's revelations!

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Have We not destroyed the earlier generations?

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: Did We not destroy the earlier people?

16.author: Arberry
16.text: Did We not destroy the ancients,

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Did We not destroy the ancients?

16.author: Itani
16.text: Did We not destroy the ancients?

16.author: Maududi
16.text: Did We not destroy many a nation of the earlier times?

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: Did We not destroy the ancients

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Destroyed We not the former folk,

16.author: Qarai
16.text: Did We not destroy the former generations,

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: Did We not destroy the ancients

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: Did We not destroy the former peoples?

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Did We not destroy the ancient people

16.author: Shakir
16.text: Did We not destroy the former generations?

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: Did We not destroy the earlier peoples?

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Did We not destroy the men of old (for their evil)?

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: So shall We make the later ones to follow them.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: We shall then send the latter after them.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: and then follow them with the later folk?

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Thereafter We shall cause to follow them the latter ones.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: So shall We make later generations to follow them.

17.author: Itani
17.text: Then succeeded them with the others?

17.author: Maududi
17.text: And We shall cause those of later times to follow them.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: So shall We make later generations to follow them.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Then caused the latter folk to follow after?

17.author: Qarai
17.text: [and] then made the latter ones follow them?

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: and let the latter follow them?

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Then We will follow them with the later ones.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: and make others settle after them in their land?

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Then did We follow them up with later ones.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: We will now cause the later ones to follow them:

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: So shall We make later (generations) follow them.

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: That is what We shall do to those who are guilty of crime.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: This is how We deal with the guilty.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: In this wise We do with the culprits.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Thus do We deal with the Mujrimun (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals, etc.)!

18.author: Itani
18.text: This is how We deal with the guilty.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: Thus do We deal with the guilty.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Thus do We deal with the criminals.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Thus deal We ever with the guilty.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: That is how We deal with the guilty.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: As such shall We deal with the sinners.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Thus do We deal with the criminals.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: Thus do We deal with the sinful ones.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Even thus shall We deal with the guilty.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: thus do We deal with the culprits.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Thus do We deal with men of sin.

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

19.author: Arberry
19.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

19.author: Itani
19.text: Woe on that Day to the rejecters.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Woe that Day to the deniers!

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: On that day, woe upon those who have rejected God's revelations!

19.author: Shakir
19.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Did We not create you from contemptible water?

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Did We not create you from an abject fluid?

20.author: Arberry
20.text: Did We not create you of a mean water,

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Created We you not of water despicable,

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Did We not create you from a worthless water (semen, etc.)?

20.author: Itani
20.text: Did We not create you from an insignificant fluid?

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Did We not create you of a mean fluid,

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Did We not create you from a despised water

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Did We not create you from a base fluid

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Have We not created you from a base fluid,

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: Did We not create you from a weak water,

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Did We not create you from a liquid disdained?

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: Did We not create you from an insignificant drop of fluid

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Did We not create you from contemptible water?

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Did We not create you from a humble fluid,

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Have We not created you from a fluid (held) despicable?-

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Then We lodged you in a secure place (the womb)

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: We then kept it in a safe place.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: that We laid within a sure lodging

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Which We placed in a depository safe.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Then We placed it in a place of safety (womb),

21.author: Itani
21.text: Then lodged it in a secure place?

21.author: Maududi
21.text: which We then placed in a secure repository

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Then We placed it in a place of safety,

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Which We laid up in a safe abode

21.author: Qarai
21.text: [and] then lodged it in a secure abode

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: which We placed within a sure lodging

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And We placed it in a firm lodging

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: and place it in a secure place

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Then We placed it in a secure resting-place,

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: then placed it in a secure repository [the womb],

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: The which We placed in a place of rest, firmly fixed,

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: For a known period (determined by gestation)?

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: For a period (of gestation), determined (according to need)?

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Then We facilitated (your birth). How well do We calculate!

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: We then calculated; so how excellently do We control!

23.author: Arberry
23.text: We determined; excellent determiners are We.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: So We decreed. How excellent are We as decreers!

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: So We did measure, and We are the Best to measure (the things).

23.author: Itani
23.text: We measured precisely. We are the best to measure.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: See that We had the power to do so. Great indeed is Our power to do what We will.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: So We did measure; and We are the best to measure.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Thus We arranged. How excellent is Our arranging!

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Then We determined; and how excellent determiners We are!

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: We determined, how excellent a Determiner are We!

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And We determined [it], and excellent [are We] to determine.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: Thus did We Plan and how excellent is Our planning!

23.author: Shakir
23.text: So We proportion it-- how well are We at proportioning (things).

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: Thus We have determined the stages of development and Our power to determine is excellent indeed.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: For We do determine (according to need); for We are the best to determine (things).

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

24.author: Arberry
24.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

24.author: Itani
24.text: Woe on that Day to the falsifiers.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Woe that Day to the deniers!

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

24.author: Qarai
24.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: On that day, woe would be upon those who have rejected the revelations of God!

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Ah woe, that Day! to the Rejecters of Truth!

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Have We not made the earth a repository

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: Did We not make the earth a storehouse?

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Made We not the earth to be a housing

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Have We not made the earth a receptacle.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Have We not made the earth a receptacle?

25.author: Itani
25.text: Did We not make the earth a homestead?

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Did We not make the earth a receptacle,

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: Have We not made the earth Kifat (a receptacle)

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Have We not made the earth a receptacle

25.author: Qarai
25.text: Have We not made the earth a receptacle

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: Have We not made the earth a housing

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: Have We not made the earth a container

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: Did We not make the earth as a gathering place

25.author: Shakir
25.text: Have We not made the earth to draw together to itself,

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Have We not made the earth a receptacle,

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Have We not made the earth (as a place) to draw together.

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: For the living and the dead,

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: For the living and the dead among you?

26.author: Arberry
26.text: for the living and for the dead?

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Both for the living and the dead,

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: For the living and the dead.

26.author: Itani
26.text: For the living and the dead?

26.author: Maududi
26.text: for the living and the dead,

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: For the living and the dead

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Both for the living and the dead,

26.author: Qarai
26.text: for the living and the dead,

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: for both the living and the dead?

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: Of the living and the dead?

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: for the living and the dead,

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: for the living and the dead?

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: And placed in it lofty and broad stablisers, and gave you sweet water to drink?

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And We placed high mountains as anchors in it and gave you sweet water to drink.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Set We not therein soaring mountains? Sated you with sweetest water?

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And have placed therein firm and tall mountains and given you to drink of water fresh

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And have placed therein firm, and tall mountains; and have given you to drink sweet water?

27.author: Itani
27.text: And set on it lofty mountains, and given you pure water to drink?

27.author: Maududi
27.text: and did We not firmly fix towering mountains on it and give you sweet water to drink?

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And have placed therein fixed towers, and have given you Furat water

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And placed therein high mountains and given you to drink sweet water therein?

27.author: Qarai
27.text: and set in it lofty [and] firm mountains, and given you agreeable water to drink?

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: Have We not placed high mountains upon it and filled you with sweet water?

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And We placed therein lofty, firmly set mountains and have given you to drink sweet water.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: place on it high mountains and provide you with fresh water?

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And made therein lofty mountains, and given you to drink of sweet water?

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: Have We not placed high mountains upon it and given you fresh water to drink?

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: And made therein mountains standing firm, lofty (in stature); and provided for you water sweet (and wholesome)?

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: WajaAAalna feeha rawasiya shamikhatin waasqaynakum maan furatan

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

28.author: Arberry
28.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

28.author: Itani
28.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: Woe that Day to the deniers!

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

28.author: Qarai
28.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: On that Day (of Judgment) woe would be upon those who have rejected God's revelations!

28.author: Shakir
28.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: "Go to what you used to deny;

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: “Move towards what you used to deny!”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Depart to that you cried was lies!

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: 'Depart Unto that which ye were wont to belie.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: (It will be said to the disbelievers): "Depart you to that which you used to deny!

29.author: Itani
29.text: “Proceed to what you used to deny.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: Proceed now towards that which you were wont to deny as false;

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: (It will be said to the disbelievers): "Depart you to that which you used to deny!"

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: (It will be said unto them:) Depart unto that (doom) which ye used to deny;

29.author: Qarai
29.text: [The faithless will be told:] ‘Proceed toward what you used to deny!

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: Depart to that which you belied!

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: [They will be told], "Proceed to that which you used to deny.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: Proceed to that (the Day of Judgment) which you have rejected.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: Walk on to that which you called a lie.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: Proceed to that which you denied.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: (It will be said:) "Depart ye to that which ye used to reject as false!

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Intaliqoo ila ma kuntum bihi tukaththiboona

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: Go to the shadow with three ramifications,

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: “Move towards the shadow of the smoke having three branches.”

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Depart Unto the shadow three branched:

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: "Depart you to a shadow (of Hell-fire smoke ascending) in three columns,

30.author: Itani
30.text: “Proceed to a shadow of three different masses.”

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: "Depart you to a shadow in three columns,"

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Depart unto the shadow falling threefold,

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: Depart into the shadow of three masses,

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: Proceed to a shadow [of smoke] having three columns

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: Proceed to that shadow, rising in three columns

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Walk on to the covering having three branches,

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: Proceed to a shadow rising in three columns:

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: "Depart ye to a Shadow (of smoke ascending) in three columns,

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Intaliqoo ila thillin thee thalathi shuAAabin

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Neither shady nor protecting against the blazing Fire.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: “Which neither gives shade, nor saves from the flame.”

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Neither shading nor availing against the flame.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: "Neither shading, nor of any use against the fierce flame of the Fire."

31.author: Itani
31.text: Offering no shade, and unavailing against the flames.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: which neither provides (cooling) shade nor protection against the flames;

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: "Neither shady nor of any use against the fierce flame of the Fire."

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: (Which yet is) no relief nor shelter from the flame.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: neither shady nor of any avail against the flames.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: wherein there is neither shade, nor freeing from the blazing flames

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: [But having] no cool shade and availing not against the flame."

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: which neither gives shade nor protects one from the flames.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Neither having the coolness of the shade nor availing against the flame.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: affording neither shade, nor protection from the flames,

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: "(Which yields) no shade of coolness, and is of no use against the fierce Blaze.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: La thaleelin wala yughnee mina allahabi

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: It will throw out sparks as logs of wood

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: Indeed hell throws up sparks like huge castles.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: that shoots sparks like dry faggots,

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: Verily it shall cast forth sparks like Unto a castle.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: Verily! It (Hell) throws sparks (huge) as Al-Qasr [a fort or a Qasr (huge log of wood)],

32.author: Maududi
32.text: it indeed throws up sparks like castles,

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: Verily, it (Hell) throws sparks (huge) as Al-Qasr,

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: Lo! it throweth up sparks like the castles,

32.author: Qarai
32.text: Indeed it throws up [giant] sparks like castles,

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: it throws spits as (high as a huge) palace,

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: Indeed, it throws sparks [as huge] as a fortress,

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: The fire will shoot out sparks as big as huge towers

32.author: Shakir
32.text: Surely it sends up sparks like palaces,

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: and throwing up sparks as huge as towers

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: "Indeed it throws about sparks (huge) as Forts,

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: As though they were camels yellow tawny.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: As if they were Jimalatun Sufr (yellow camels or bundles of ropes)."

33.author: Itani
33.text: As if they were yellow camels.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: which seem as though they are yellow-coloured camels.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: As if they were Sufr camels.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: (Or) as it might be camels of bright yellow hue.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: As if they were yellowish [black] camels.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: As if they were tawny camels.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: and as bright as a herd of yellow camels.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: "As if there were (a string of) yellow camels (marching swiftly)."

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

34.author: Itani
34.text: Woe on that Day to the liars.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

34.author: Qarai
34.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: On that day, woe would be upon those who have rejected God's revelations!

34.author: Shakir
34.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: They will not be able to speak that day,

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: This is a day in which they will not be able to speak.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: This is the day they shall not speak

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: This is a Day whereon they shall not be able to speak.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: That will be a Day when they shall not speak (during some part of it),

35.author: Itani
35.text: This is a Day when they will not speak.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: That will be the Day on which they will not (be able to) utter a word,

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: That will be a Day when they shall not speak (during some part of it),

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: This is a day wherein they speak not,

35.author: Qarai
35.text: This is a day wherein they will not speak,

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: This is the Day they shall not speak,

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: This is a Day they will not speak,

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: On that day they will not be able to speak,

35.author: Shakir
35.text: This is the day on which they shall not speak,

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: On that Day they will be speechless,

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: That will be a Day when they shall not be able to speak.

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: Nor given leave to make excuses.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: Nor will they be given permission to present excuses.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: neither be given leave, and excuse themselves.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: Nor shall they be permitted so that they might excuse themselves.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And they will not be permitted to put forth any excuse.

36.author: Itani
36.text: And they will not be allowed to apologize.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: nor will they be allowed to proffer excuses.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And they will not be permitted to put forth any excuse.

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: Nor are they suffered to put forth excuses.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: nor will they be permitted to offer excuses.

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: nor shall they be given permission, so that they can apologize.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: Nor will it be permitted for them to make an excuse.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: nor will they be permitted to offer any excuses.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And permission shall not be given to them so that they should offer excuses.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: nor shall they be permitted to offer excuses.

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Nor will it be open to them to put forth pleas.

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

37.author: Itani
37.text: Woe on that Day to the rejecters.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

37.author: Qarai
37.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: On that day, woe would be those who have rejected God's revelations!

37.author: Shakir
37.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: That will be the Day of Reckoning. Gathered will be (you) and the earlier (generations).

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: This is the Day of Decision; We have gathered you and all the earlier men.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: 'This is the Day of Decision; We have joined you with the ancients;

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: This is the Day of Decision, We have assembled both ye and the ancients.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: That will be a Day of Decision! We have brought you and the men of old together!

38.author: Itani
38.text: This is the Day of Separation; We have gathered you, together with the ancients.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: That is the Day of Judgement on which We have assembled you as well as all those who went before you.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: That will be a Day of Decision! We have brought you and the men of old together!

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: This is the Day of Decision, We have brought you and the men of old together.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: ‘This is the Day of Judgement. We have brought you together with the former generations.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: Such is the Day of Decision. We will gather you with the ancients.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: This is the Day of Judgement; We will have assembled you and the former peoples.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: That is the Day of Judgment. We will bring you together with all the ancient peoples.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: This is the day of decision: We have gathered you and those of yore.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: This is the Day of Judgement. We have assembled you all together with past generations.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: That will be a Day of Sorting out! We shall gather you together and those before (you)!

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: So if you have any plot to devise against Me, then devise it.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: If you now have any conspiracy, carry it out on Me.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: if you have a trick, try you now to trick Me!'

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: If now ye have any craft, try that craft upon Me.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: So if you have a plot, use it against Me (Allah)!

39.author: Itani
39.text: So if you have a strategy, use it against Me.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: So if you have any ploy, try it against Me!

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: So, if you have a plot, use it against Me (Allah)!

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: If now ye have any wit, outwit Me.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: If you have any stratagems [left], try them out against Me!’

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: If you are cunning, then try your cunning against Me!

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: So if you have a plan, then plan against Me.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: If you have any plans, use them.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: So if you have a plan, plan against Me (now).

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: If now you have any strategy, use it against Me.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: Now, if ye have a trick (or plot), use it against Me!

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Woe on the day Unto the beliers!

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

40.author: Itani
40.text: Woe on that Day to the falsifiers.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: On that day, woe would be upon those who have rejected God's revelations!

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: Those who take heed for themselves and fear God, will be amidst shade and springs of water,

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: Indeed the pious are in shade and springs.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: Truly the godfearing shall dwell amid shades and fountains,

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: Verily the God-fearing shall be amid shades and springs,

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: Verily, the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2) shall be amidst shades and springs.

41.author: Itani
41.text: The righteous will be amidst shades and fountains.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: Behold, today the God-fearing will be amidst shades and springs,

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: Verily, those who had Taqwa, shall be amidst shades and springs.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: Lo! those who kept their duty are amid shade and fountains,

41.author: Qarai
41.text: Indeed the Godwary will be amid shades and springs

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Indeed, the cautious, shall live amid shades and fountains

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: Indeed, the righteous will be among shades and springs

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: The pious ones will rest amid the shade, springs,

41.author: Shakir
41.text: Surely those who guard (against evil) shall be amid shades and fountains,

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: The righteous shall dwell amidst cool shades and fountains,

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: As to the Righteous, they shall be amidst (cool) shades and springs (of water).

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: And such fruits as they desire.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: And among fruits whichever they may desire.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: and such fruits as their hearts desire:

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: And fruits such as they shall desire.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: And fruits, such as they desire.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: and the fruits that they desire (will be ready at hand).

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: And fruits, such as they desire.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: And fruits such as they desire.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: and [enjoying] such fruits as they desire.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: and have such fruits as they desire.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: And fruits from whatever they desire,

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: and fruits of the kind which they desire.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: And fruits such as they desire.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: and shall have fruits such as they desire;

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: And (they shall have) fruits,- all they desire.

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: "Eat and drink with relish as reward for what you had done."

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: “Eat and drink with pleasure, the reward of your deeds.”

43.author: Arberry
43.text: 'Eat and drink, with wholesome appetite, for that you were working.'

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: 'Eat and drink with relish that which ye have been working.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: "Eat and drink comfortably for that which you used to do.

43.author: Itani
43.text: “Eat and drink pleasantly, for what you used to do.”

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Eat and drink and may every joy attend you as a reward for your deeds.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: "Eat and drink comfortably for that which you used to do."

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: (Unto them it is said:) Eat, drink and welcome, O ye blessed, in return for what ye did.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: [They will be told:] ‘Enjoy your food and drink, [a reward] for what you used to do.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: (It shall be said to them): 'Eat and drink, with a good appetite, for all that you did'

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: [Being told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you used to do."

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: (They will be told), "Eat and drink in good health as a reward for what you have done".

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Eat and drink pleasantly because of what you did.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: [They will be told], "Eat and drink with relish in return for what you did [in life]:

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: "Eat ye and drink ye to your heart's content: for that ye worked (Righteousness).

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Kuloo waishraboo haneean bima kuntum taAAmaloona

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: That is how We reward the good.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: This is how We reward the virtuous.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Even so do We recompense the good-doers.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Verily We! in this wise We recompense the well-doers.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: Verily, thus We reward the Muhsinun (good-doers).

44.author: Itani
44.text: This is how We reward the doers of good.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Thus do We reward those that do good.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: Verily, thus We reward the Muhsinin.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Thus do We reward the good.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Thus do We reward the virtuous.’

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: As such We recompense those who did good.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: Thus do We reward the righteous ones.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Surely thus do We reward the doers of good.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: this is how We reward those who do good."

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: Thus do We certainly reward the Doers of Good.

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

45.author: Itani
45.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: Woe that Day to the deniers!

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: On that day, woe would be upon those who have rejected God's revelations!

45.author: Shakir
45.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: [But] woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Eat and enjoy for a while: You are certainly sinners.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: “Eat and enjoy for a while – indeed you are guilty.”

46.author: Arberry
46.text: 'Eat and take your joy a little; you are sinners!'

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: 'Eat and enjoy a little; verily ye are culprits.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: (O you disbelievers)! Eat and enjoy yourselves (in this worldly life) for a little while. Verily, you are the Mujrimun (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals, etc.).

46.author: Itani
46.text: “Eat and enjoy yourselves a little; you are indeed criminals.”

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Eat and enjoy yourselves for a while. Surely you are evil-doers.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: Eat and enjoy yourselves for a little while. Verily, you are criminals.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: Eat and take your ease (on earth) a little. Lo! ye are guilty.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: ‘Eat and enjoy a little! You are indeed guilty.’

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: 'Eat and enjoy yourselves a little, for you are sinners'

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: [O disbelievers], eat and enjoy yourselves a little; indeed, you are criminals.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: (Disbelievers), eat and enjoy yourselves for a little while. You are certainly sinful ones.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: Eat and enjoy yourselves for a little; surely you are guilty.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: Eat [your fill] and enjoy your life for a little while, O you who are lost in sin.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: (O ye unjust!) Eat ye and enjoy yourselves (but) a little while, for that ye are Sinners.

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

47.author: Arberry
47.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

47.author: Itani
47.text: Woe on that Day to the liars.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: Woe that Day to the deniers!

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

47.author: Qarai
47.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: On that day, woe would be upon those who have rejected God's revelations!

47.author: Shakir
47.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: When it is said to them: "Bow in homage," they do not bow.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And when it is said to them, “Offer the prayer” – they do not!

48.author: Arberry
48.text: When it is said to them, 'Prostrate yourselves!' they prostrate not.

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And when it is said Unto them: 'bow down, they bow not down.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: And when it is said to them: "Bow down yourself (in prayer)!" They bow not down (offer not their prayers).

48.author: Itani
48.text: And when it is said to them, “Kneel”, they do not kneel.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: When it is said to them: “Bow down (before Allah),” they do not bow down.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And when it is said to them: "Bow down yourself!" They bow not down.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: When it is said unto them: Bow down, they bow not down!

48.author: Qarai
48.text: When they are told, ‘Bow down [in prayer],’ they do not bow down!

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: When it is said to them: 'Bow yourselves' they do not bow.

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And when it is said to them, "Bow [in prayer]," they do not bow.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: When they are told to say their prayers, they do not bow down (in prayer).

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And where it is said to them: Bow down, they do not bow down.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: When they are bidden to bow down, they do not bow down.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: And when it is said to them, "Prostrate yourselves!" they do not so.

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Waitha qeela lahumu irkaAAoo la yarkaAAoona

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Alas the woe that day for those who deny!

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

49.author: Arberry
49.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies!

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Woe on that day Unto the beliers!

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)!

49.author: Itani
49.text: Woe on that Day to the rejecters.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: Woe on that Day to those that give the lie to the Truth!

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Woe that Day to the deniers!

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Woe to the deniers on that day!

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it!

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: On that day, woe would be upon those who have rejected God's revelations!

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: Woe on that Day to those who reject the truth!

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Ah woe, that Day, to the Rejecters of Truth!

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: In what other lore after this will they then believe?

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: So after this, in what matter will they believe?

50.author: Arberry
50.text: In what discourse after this will they believe?

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: In what discourse, then, after it, will they believe?

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: Then in what statement after this (the Quran) will they believe?

50.author: Itani
50.text: In what message, beyond this, will they believe?

50.author: Maududi
50.text: In what discourse after this (Qur'an) will they, then, believe?

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: Then in what statement after this will they believe

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: In what statement, after this, will they believe?

50.author: Qarai
50.text: So what discourse will they believe after this?

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: After this, in what discourse will they believe?

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: Then in what statement after the Qur'an will they believe?

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: In which word other than the Quran will they believe?

50.author: Shakir
50.text: In what announcement, then, after it, will they believe?

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: In which word then, after this, will they believe?

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: Then what Message, after that, will they believe in?
